metadata.title: Weigh a Black Hole by Watching One Star
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = stellar orbit / Kepler's law / mass from motion; CRIMSON = invisible mass / empty sky / nothing visible
metadata.note: Exclusions honored: no EHT photo detail, no proper motion explanation, no Nobel prize biographies. Wonder register.

beats.narration_text: med davy A-I. From Sanskrit — meaning intelligent, or intellectually brilliant. The perfect name for our A-I-powered intelligent learning systems.

props.headline: Medhavy AI
props.body:
मेधावी (Medhavy): From Sanskrit, meaning
"intelligent" or "intellectually brilliant"
— the perfect name for our AI-powered
intelligent learning systems.

beats.narration_text: At the galactic center, one star traces a visible oval every 16 years around an apparently empty patch of sky. That oval alone encodes 4 million solar masses of invisible nothing. This is Sagittarius A-star.

card.copy: Weigh a Black Hole by Watching One Star

beats.narration_text: Kepler's third law: the square of an orbital period equals the cube of the semi-major axis divided by the central mass. P squared equals a cubed over M. Three observables. Measure two, solve for the third.

production_viz.label: P² = a³/M equation displayed; three labeled variables: period, semi-major axis, central mass; TEAL for Kepler's law

beats.narration_text: How do you weigh an object you can't see, in a region of space you can never visit, from a single visible star's orbit?

card.copy: Can't see it. Can't visit. One star tracing an oval every 16 years. How do you get 4 million solar masses from that?

beats.narration_text: The star S0-2 orbits Sagittarius A-star with a period of about 16 years and a semi-major axis of about 1000 astronomical units — measured from the angular size of the orbit and the known distance to the galactic center.

production_viz.label: S0-2 oval orbit with period 16 yr and semi-major axis 1000 AU labeled; TEAL for measured orbit; empty center for invisible BH in CRIMSON

beats.narration_text: Apply Kepler's third law: M equals a cubed divided by P squared. With a in astronomical units and P in years, in units where solar mass equals 1: M comes out to roughly 4 million solar masses.

production_viz.label: M = a³/P² calculation shown: (1000)³/(16)² = 10^9/256 ≈ 4×10^6 M_sun; TEAL for result

beats.narration_text: The galactic center is 8 kiloparsecs — about 26,000 light-years — away. The angular size of the orbit is measured in arcseconds. The small-angle formula converts: angular size in arcseconds times distance in parsecs gives the physical size in AU.

production_viz.label: Small-angle conversion diagram: arcsec × 8000 pc = AU; 8 kpc distance labeled; TEAL for conversion formula

beats.narration_text: Multiple stars orbit the same point — S0-2, S0-38, S0-102, and many others. Each gives its own independent mass estimate. They all agree. The confidence comes from consistency across many independent measurements.

production_viz.label: Multiple star orbits (S0-2, S0-38, S0-102) all centered on same empty point; consistent mass estimate arrows in TEAL

beats.narration_text: The 2020 Nobel Prize in Physics was awarded partly for this observation. Andrea Ghez and Reinhard Genzel led teams tracking stars at the galactic center over decades, each independently confirming the 4-million-solar-mass result.

production_viz.label: Timeline of galactic center observations decades long; Nobel 2020 marker; two independent teams shown converging on same result in TEAL

beats.narration_text: The Event Horizon Telescope imaged Sagittarius A-star in 2022 — a direct photograph of the black hole's shadow at the scale predicted by its mass. Kepler's third law measurement agreed with the image within uncertainties.

production_viz.label: EHT shadow size prediction from Kepler's law vs actual image scale; agreement arrow in TEAL; 2022 label

beats.narration_text: S0-2: period approximately 16 years, semi-major axis approximately 1000 AU. Kepler's third law: M equals a cubed over P squared gives approximately 4 million solar masses. Nobel Prize 2020. EHT 2022 confirmed.

card.copy: P² = a³/M. S0-2: T ≈ 16 yr, a ≈ 1000 AU → M ≈ 4M solar masses = Sgr A*. 2020 Nobel Prize. 2022 EHT image confirmed.

beats.narration_text: med davy. A-I-powered intelligent learning systems. On YouTube, at med davy A-I. med davy dot com.
